comments.body: Implemented in strict migration order + pushed as 3 stacked Gerrit changes: 456 (docs: CONTRIBUTING DCO section w/ developercertificate.org/commit -s/not-a-CLA/amend runbook, CLAUDE.md exact agent trailer, tutorial retarget), 457 (e2e -s: reviewbot + scenarios commit -s + merge --signoff + feature-branch-e2e.sh prepare-commit-msg hook), 458 (project.config [receive] requireSignedOffBy=true + rollback note). Migration order git-provable: config commit is a descendant of both e2e and docs commits. GitHub web_commit_signoff_required flipped true. REMAINING (deferred to end, per spec): setup-project.sh server rollout + live verification (unsigned push rejected -> amend -s accepted) execute ONLY after 456+457+458 MERGE to main, so no push is rejected mid-migration. All static ACs grep-verified; bash -n + config-check green.

compiled_state.description:
# Adopt the DCO: declarative Gerrit enforcement, contributor docs, agent sign-off policy

## Why

rebar has no inbound-IP mechanism (no DCO, no CLA). Both external reports and peer convergence recommend DCO over CLA: Qt's CLA is the stated reason it cannot accept PRs, and OpenStack replaced its CLA with the DCO for all contributions (TC resolution 2025-05-20, https://governance.openstack.org/tc/resolutions/20250520-replace-the-cla-with-dco-for-all-contributions.html) explicitly to lower the barrier, directing enforcement into Gerrit itself — the server rejects an unsigned push at `refs/for/*` time, zero new infrastructure. Gerrit documents `receive.requireSignedOffBy` as a per-project `project.config` key (https://gerrit-review.googlesource.com/Documentation/config-project-config.html#receive.requireSignedOffBy — inheritable, NOT site-only); live verification below guards against placement surprises anyway.

Sequencing note (resolves the file-existence review findings): `docs/your-first-change.md` does not exist at plan time — it is created by story **fat-leaflike-canary**, which this story `depends_on` (edge recorded in the store). Every edit this story makes to that file executes only after that story lands; in dependency order the target exists.

## What

- **Declarative config path (never a manual push):** add `requireSignedOffBy = true` to the `[receive]` section of `infra/gerrit/project.config` and roll out via the existing `setup-project.sh` flow to `refs/meta/config` (JoeOakhartNava admin identity). Rationale: `setup-project.sh` overwrites `project.config` on every run; a one-off manual push would be silently reverted. **Rollback:** revert the line, re-run `setup-project.sh` — documented in a comment beside the key.
- **Two distinct sign-off rules (resolves the identity-mismatch finding):**
  1. **Agent commits (RebarBotNava / `joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` committer):** trailer is exactly `Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart <joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com>` — the responsible human's real name with his registered plus-addressed mailbox (routes to the human; kernel practice accepts plus-addresses; the certifier is the human, satisfying DCO 1.1). Documented in CLAUDE.md's commit guidance verbatim.
  2. **e2e test scripts** (`infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e.sh`, `infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e.sh`, `infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh` — all push to `refs/for/*` and would be rejected post-flip): each script adds `-s` to its `git commit` invocations so the sign-off is the script's OWN configured test identity (a hardcoded human trailer would mismatch the test committer). No other trailer engineering.
- **Automation inventory (decision rule):** enumerate commit-creating automation with `rg -l "git commit" infra/ scripts/ .github/ Makefile` at execution; the three e2e scripts above are the known set; anything newly found is updated the same way (add `-s`) or ticketed via discovered_from.
- CONTRIBUTING.md "Sign your work (DCO)" section, moby/Docker template (https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md "Sign your work"): link developercertificate.org, show the trailer, `git commit -s`, real-name requirement, push-time rejection note, Envoy-style "fixing DCO" note (amend/rebase re-signing), the shepherded-patch two-trailer rule (contributor's own sign-off required in the patch; shepherd adds theirs on amend — coordinated with the PR-posture story), **and a one-line CLA decision record: "rebar uses the DCO, not a CLA; a CLA would be adopted only if Nava counsel requires rights beyond Apache-2.0 §5 + DCO"** (the durable in-repo record of that decision).
- Cross-link retarget INTO `docs/your-first-change.md` (after fat-leaflike-canary lands — see sequencing note): point the tutorial's interim developercertificate.org link at the new CONTRIBUTING DCO section.
- **Migration order (strict, and provable from git history, not tracker artifacts):** commits land in this order — (1) docs (CONTRIBUTING/CLAUDE.md/tutorial retarget); (2) e2e-script `-s` fixes; (3) the `infra/gerrit/project.config` flag + `setup-project.sh` rollout. Because all three are commits in this repo, the order is verifiable post-hoc: the commit touching `infra/gerrit/project.config` must be a descendant of the commits touching the e2e scripts and docs (`git log --oneline -- <path>` comparison). No window where agent/bot pushes fail.
- **Post-flip failure runbook** (one paragraph in the CONTRIBUTING DCO section): rejected push → `git commit --amend -s --no-edit`, re-push; agent-tooling regression → roll back the flag while fixing.
- Flip GitHub's "require sign-off on web-based commits" on the mirror (one settings toggle; belt-and-braces).
- Peer-norm citation for the human-signs policy: Kubernetes AI-review governance (https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/github-management/ai-code-review-tools.md) — cited as supporting practice, not load-bearing authority (the policy stands on DCO 1.1's own terms).

## Scope

IN: `infra/gerrit/project.config` + setup-project.sh rollout, rollback note, CONTRIBUTING.md DCO section (incl. CLA record + failure runbook), CLAUDE.md agent-trailer guidance, e2e-script `-s` fixes, tutorial cross-link retarget (post-dependency), GitHub web-signoff toggle, live verification.
OUT: CLA machinery; the probot/dco GitHub app (acts on PR checks; PRs here are answered/closed by the lockdown workflow the PR-posture story lands); retrofitting existing history (DCO applies to new commits only — kernel/OpenStack precedent); CLAUDE.local.md (gitignored, not a reviewable artifact).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Config flag present and rolled out — prove: `grep -A5 "\[receive\]" infra/gerrit/project.config | grep -q "requireSignedOffBy = true"`; the rollback note exists — prove: `grep -qi "rollback" infra/gerrit/project.config`.
- [ ] Live verification: an unsigned push to `refs/for/main` is rejected (server message captured in the commit message or a `docs/` note) and the same commit re-pushed after `git commit --amend -s` is accepted.
- [ ] e2e scripts sign their commits — prove, per file: `grep -q "commit .*-s\b\|commit -s" infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e.sh`; same grep for `infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e.sh` and `infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh` (three separate checks); one e2e suite run passes post-flip.
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md DCO section — prove: `grep -q "developercertificate.org" CONTRIBUTING.md`; `grep -q "commit -s" CONTRIBUTING.md`; `grep -qi "not a CLA" CONTRIBUTING.md` (the CLA decision record); `grep -qi "amend" CONTRIBUTING.md` (fix-up/runbook).
- [ ] CLAUDE.md names the exact agent trailer — prove: `grep -q "Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart <joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com>" CLAUDE.md`.
- [ ] Migration order provable from history — prove: the commit touching `infra/gerrit/project.config` is a descendant of the latest commits touching the three e2e scripts and the CONTRIBUTING DCO section (compare `git log` for the paths).
- [ ] Tutorial retarget done after dependency — prove: `grep -q "Sign your work\|DCO" docs/your-first-change.md` links the CONTRIBUTING section (executes post fat-leaflike-canary).
- [ ] GitHub web-signoff on — prove: `gh api repos/navapbc/rebar --jq .web_commit_signoff_required` = true.
compiled_state.title: Adopt the DCO: Gerrit server-side enforcement, contributor docs, agent sign-off policy
